title: How Naming One Impulse Out Loud Rewires the Next Ten
caption:
Self-control isn't willpower. Scripture calls it a fruit of the Spirit, not a personality trait some guys have and some don't.

It starts small. The urge doesn't ask permission, but it loses power the second you call it by its actual name, not "just tired" or "just bored." The self-controlled man wins the fight at 9 AM, not 11 PM, because he decides in the quiet what he'll do in the noise.

"Whoever is faithful in a very little is also faithful in much." (Luke 16:10) One impulse named and starved makes the next one easier to catch. You don't need a clean streak. Let today's small win be today's whole win.

He's not managing the impulse forever. He's putting it to death, one named moment at a time.

Save this and name the one thing before it names you tonight.

#faith #christianmen #selfcontrol #godlymen #manofgod #scripture #discipline
theme: He runs his life — his impulses don't control him.
